rulings.Case Title: BETTY WILLIAMS, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al VS. DOES 1 THROUGH 75, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Autozone West, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issue 2 (all causes of action based on the statute of limitations) and granted as to Issue 3 (punitive damages). Defendant failed to demonstrate that the survival and wrongful death causes of action are time-barred. As to the survival causes of action, defendant failed to demonstrate that they are time-barred by C.C.P. 366.1. As to the wrongful death causes of action, defendant failed to demonstrate they are time-barred by C.C.P. 340.2: Plaintiffs' initial complaint was filed within the statute of limitations period. Defendant failed to demonstrate that the amendment substituting defendant for a Doe does not relate back to the filing of the initial complaint because it failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs were not genuinely ignorant of defendant's true name or the facts rendering it liable when the complaint was filed. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to submit clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. The Court will consider plaintiffs' opposition. . If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
